records.ety: [For incenser, fr. OF. encensier, F. encensoir, fr. LL. incensarium, incensorium, fr. L. incensum incense. See Incense, and cf. Incensory.]

defs: A vessel for perfumes; esp. one in which incense is burned.
quotes: Her thoughts are like the fume of frankincense Which from a golden censer forth doth rise.
notes: ☞ The ecclesiastical censer is usually cup-shaped, has a cover pierced with holes, and is hung by chains. The censer bearer swings it to quicken the combustion.
